name: Warehouse (PyPI.org upstream software)
vendor: Python Software Foundation / Python Packaging Authority (PyPA)
cost: Free and open source (Apache-2.0). PyPI.org is free to use for both publishers and consumers. PyPI Organization accounts are free for qualifying Community organizations (open-source projects under OSI-approved licenses); Corporate ("Company") organizations are a paid tier documented at $5 per member user per month, invoiced monthly with self-serve billing.
language: Python (Pyramid web framework, SQLAlchemy ORM, PostgreSQL, Jinja2 templates, Celery workers; deployed with Gunicorn behind the Fastly CDN)
perf_notes: Designed to run behind the Fastly CDN, which absorbs the bulk of read traffic; the origin is a stateless Pyramid/Gunicorn app tier. PyPI serves on the order of billions of file downloads per day and petabytes of bandwidth per month, but almost all of that is CDN cache hits, not origin work. No official per-request latency/throughput benchmarks are published; real-world performance is dominated by CDN cache-hit ratio rather than the application itself.
scale_notes: Runs as a highly-available production service at pypi.org: stateless horizontally-scaled web tier behind Fastly, PostgreSQL (primary + read replicas), Redis for caching/sessions, object storage (S3/GCS/B2) for files, and Celery + queue workers for async jobs (download-log ingestion via Linehaul to BigQuery, email, malware checks). Hosts ~650k+ projects and millions of releases. The software is self-hostable in principle but is built and operated for the single canonical PyPI deployment, not packaged for general self-hosting.
summary: Warehouse is the open-source Python/Pyramid application that powers PyPI.org, the canonical public Python package index, with upload, simple/JSON APIs, trusted publishing, attestations, and organization accounts.
extras: Mandatory two-factor auth (TOTP + WebAuthn/FIDO2 hardware security keys) for all accounts that own or maintain projects
API tokens are macaroons: attenuable, project-scoped, with constant-time verification
Trusted Publishing (OIDC) across GitHub Actions, GitLab.com CI/CD, Google Cloud, and ActiveState
PEP 740 provenance/attestations served as JSON 'provenance' objects rooted in Sigstore + Trusted Publisher identity (live since Nov 2024, 20k+ attestations within weeks)

PEP 770 SBOM files embedded in uploaded wheels (.dist-info/sboms/) are validated for presence/format/conformance on upload (PEP Final Apr 2025) and travel inside the distribution; PyPI does not itself generate SBOMs or expose a standalone SBOM export endpoint, so sbom support is partial
Organization accounts with teams, owner/manager/member roles, and org-level trusted publishers; Community orgs free, Corporate orgs $5/member/month
Project Quarantine: admins/security can hide and block install of suspected-malware projects without irrevocable deletion
Linehaul streams download logs to the public Google BigQuery dataset; PyPI Stats JSON API + /stats/ page for aggregate stats
Per-account and per-project security/audit history (JournalEntry events)
readme_renderer renders project descriptions (Markdown/reST) on project pages
GPG/PGP signature uploads were removed in 2023; signing is now via Sigstore-backed attestations
Legacy XML-RPC search API is permanently disabled; only the web-UI search at /search/ remains, so search_api is partial
Application logs are structured JSON via structlog (warehouse/logging.py, JSONRenderer)
Continuously deployed from main (no versioned releases); repo version stays pinned
